---
title: "Untitled"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library('tidyverse')
set.seed(123)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Data Preprocessing

```{r}
tplant <- read_csv(
  file = "data/tplant_observations - main_2025-05-05.csv",
  na = c("Na", "n", "na"),
  col_types = cols(
    ID = col_character(),
    Tray = col_factor(),
    Row = col_integer(),
    Column = col_integer(),
    n_shoot_axis = col_integer(),
    Leaf_shape = col_factor(),
    Leaf_edge = col_factor(),
    Comments = col_character()
  )
) |>
  filter(!(is.na(ID))) |> # remove plants without ID/label
  mutate(
    # change pop 165 to 156
    ID = case_when(ID == "165-1" ~ "156-1",
                   ID == "165-2" ~ "156-2",
                   ID == "165-3" ~ "156-3",
                   TRUE ~ ID),
    Pop = stringi::stri_extract(ID, regex = "^.*(?=-)"),
    # extract Population number from the ID
    Replicate = stringi::stri_extract(ID, regex = "(?<=-).*$"),
    # extract Replicate number from the ID
    Leaf_shape = case_when(Leaf_shape == "o" ~ "O", Leaf_shape == "l" ~ "L", TRUE ~ Leaf_shape),
    Leaf_edge = case_when(Leaf_edge == "s" ~ "S", Leaf_edge == "g" ~ "G", TRUE ~ Leaf_edge)
  ) |>
  select(ID, Pop, Replicate, everything()) 
```

```{r}
ID_is_not_alive_A_tha <- c(
  "384-20",
  "486-7",
  "463-5",
  "384-12",
  "346-15",
  "384-15",
  "472-6",
  "408-5",
  "408-9",
  "346-16",
  "482-5",
  "247-1",
  "465-1",
  "409-12",
  "409-18",
  "409-19",
  "409-5",
  "409-14",
  "494-18",
  "406-15",
  "406-17",
  "438-9",
  "466-21",
  "346-14",
  "463-1",
  "345-7",
  "471-8",
  "452-5",
  "406-18",
  "452-8",
  "345-10",
  "342-13",
  "406-19",
  "406-20",
  "342-12",
  "345-20",
  "159-5",
  "459-13",
  "451-16",
  "375-1",
  "471-9",
  "475-3",
  "45-18",
  "471-5",
  "344-10",
  "471-6",
  "454-1",
  "7-5",
  "375-5",
  "487-10",
  "479-20",
  "468-1",
  "375-4",
  "442-4",
  "408-8",
  "346-8",
  "409-13",
  "375-2",
  "346-12",
  "480-10",
  "346-13",
  "409-6",
  "409-20",
  "205-1",
  "470-3",
  "34-16",
  "176-2",
  "360-1",
  "160-2",
  "345-4",
  "345-13",
  "472-13",
  "345-18",
  "213-6",
  "494-19",
  "486-16",
  "494-17",
  "347-17",
  "104-9",
  "213-4",
  "408-7",
  "205-5",
  "184-1",
  "187-2",
  "433-1",
  "475-6",
  "409-10",
  "494-16",
  "409-7",
  "180-8",
  "488-19",
  "409-9",
  "409-8",
  "409-17",
  "267-21",
  "407-14",
  "465-2",
  "468-2",
  "494-7",
  "100-1",
  "348-18",
  "7-17",
  "468-15",
  "491-4",
  "406-10",
  "493-1",
  "308-6",
  "308-8",
  "308-14",
  "308-19",
  "310-1",
  "315-3",
  "308-10",
  "308-4",
  "308-3",
  "310-19"
)

is_below_18cm <- read_csv(file = "data/F5_below_18cm.csv")

Pop_pool_sampled <- c("1", "7", "8", "24", "27", "34", "35", "41", "45", "47", "49", "102", "104", "105", "113", "122", "159", "168", "169", "172", "173", "177", "180", "183", "184", "203", "232", "233", "258", "263", "264", "265", "267", "282", "310", "342", "344", "345", "346", "347", "348", "357", "359", "384", "405", "406", "408", "428", "433", "438", "439", "447", "448", "449", "451", "453", "458", "459", "466", "468", "472", "474", "477", "478", "479", "480", "481", "482", "483", "484", "486", "487", "488", "489", "491", "494")

Pop_indv_sampled <- c("19", "24", "29", "41", "45", "94", "105", "106", "113", "120", "122", "161", "167", "177", "182", "187", "191", "205", "206", "213", "233", "241", "255", "264", "357", "359", "361", "375", "407", "409", "424", "429", "438", "441", "444", "450", "452", "454", "456", "462", "463", "469", "470", "471", "475", "476")
# Pop 409 changed from Pool to individual sampled, sampled 1, 2, 3, 4, 11; Pop 407 changed from Pool to individual

#ID_skipped_sampling <- c("172-5", "180-3", "180-4", "180-7", "180-11", "184-1", "184-5", "122-18", "122-20", "408-7", "346-16", "408-7", "120-2", "182-4", "205-1", "205-5", "24-5", "24-12", "24-18", "24-20", "1-13", "7-5", "7-8", "45-8", "45-10", "45-11", "45-12", "45-20", "159-3", "159-4", "159-9", "159-10", "159-13", "168-12", "168-14", "168-16", "177-4", "177-8", "177-9", "177-10", "177-12", "177-13", "177-14", "177-16", "177-18", "177-20", "233-7", "468-12", "408-3")

# the column sampletype shows the assigned type of sampling per population, but
# gives no information if the replicate was sampled or not
tplant <- tplant |>
  mutate(
    is_alive_A_tha = case_when(!(ID %in% ID_is_not_alive_A_tha) ~ "TRUE", TRUE ~ "FALSE"),
    sampletype = case_when(
      Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled &
        Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled ~ "Pool_and_Indv",
      Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled &
        !(Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled) ~ "Pool",
      !(Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled) &
        Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled ~ "Indv",
      TRUE ~ "Not_sampled"
    ),
    is_above_18cm = case_when(ID %in% is_below_18cm$F5_is_below_18cm ~ FALSE,
                              TRUE ~ TRUE)
  )

  
```

```{r}
# Summing up replicates for each population
summed_pop <- tplant |>
  filter(is_alive_A_tha == TRUE)|>
  group_by(Pop) |>
  summarise(total_repl = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(total_repl))

summed_pop
```

```{r}
# add label for threshold of total replicats above 5
summed_pop_above_3 <- summed_pop |>
  mutate(pop_above_3 = ifelse(total_repl > 3, T, F))

summed_pop_above_3
```

```{r}
summed_pop_above_3 |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == T) |>
  nrow()
```

Out of the 164 populations 112 have more than 3 replicates, 54 less.

The following Population have not 4 replicates.

```{r}
# Populations below threshold of replicates
tplant_pop_below_3 <- summed_pop_above_3 |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == F) 

tplant_pop_below_3$Pop
```

```{r}
# Add the above to the tplant df
tplant <- tplant |>
  group_by(Pop) |>
  filter(is_alive_A_tha == TRUE)|>
  mutate(total_repl_in_pop = n()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(pop_above_3 = ifelse(total_repl_in_pop > 3, T, F),
         pop_below_15 = ifelse(total_repl_in_pop < 15, T, F)) |>
  relocate(Comments, .after = last_col())


tplant
```

## Generating Table for Dormancy
Initial random subsampling:
```{r}
# dormancy2 <- tplant |>
#   filter(pop_above_3 == TRUE & is_alive_A_tha == TRUE & !(Tray == 37)) |>
#   filter(Pop != 167) |>
#   group_by(Pop) |>
#   slice_sample(n = 3)

# saved as "dormancy_table_2025-05-08.csv"

# dormancy_selected_table_2025_05_14 <- data.frame(
#   ID = c("113-7", "486-1", "346-7", "429-5", "462-1", "484-7", "94-3", "444-3", "263-4", "105-10", "310-7", "232-14", "468-9", "282-19", "345-11", "264-8", "29-5", "458-4", "477-6", "479-11", "203-9", "451-7", "453-11", "452-1", "452-7", "433-15", "8-14", "424-4", "439-1", "8-9", "359-8", "474-4", "35-5", "452-9", "405-6", "454-6", "458-1", "468-6", "114-2", "361-5", "357-11", "165-17", "482-11", "114-3", "113-8", "106-3", "407-17", "172-15", "444-1", "463-2", "453-2", "258-3", "105-6", "173-6", "104-17", "102-11", "106-4", "94-1", "463-10", "264-11", "232-18", "348-12", "168-10", "172-12", "203-3", "159-6", "182-1", "447-9", "449-4", "49-5", "408-2", "47-17", "347-8", "169-12", "406-6", "407-1", "187-4", "187-6", "180-6", "182-3", "454-2", "120-4", "263-6", "448-7", "439-4", "459-9", "441-3", "484-16", "448-6", "449-9", "165-4", "408-4", "241-7", "310-11", "282-4", "253-3", "265-19", "357-4", "453-15", "469-3", "481-14", "348-14", "347-16", "177-16", "187-5", "191-4", "478-15", "342-10", "476-4", "477-14", "494-2", "310-13", "24-11", "102-2", "213-2", "409-2", "424-3", "428-17", "481-4", "255-1", "267-19", "265-16", "8-15", "491-17", "233-17", "45-6", "456-4", "456-5", "406-9", "433-13", "232-8", "447-4", "173-13", "161-3", "491-14", "459-3", "477-3", "167-2", "474-14", "475-4", "429-4", "468-12", "487-7", "482-15", "428-7", "428-2", "483-6", "476-6", "102-12", "19-3", "1-10", "165-8", "359-4", "168-11", "183-15", "346-10", "346-20", "106-2", "405-17", "344-19", "120-1", "489-13", "488-5", "45-4", "459-2", "408-10", "475-5", "463-8", "7-3", "24-7", "47-12", "405-10", "447-6", "241-6", "488-18", "265-10", "177-4", "49-1", "462-2", "345-5", "41-8", "180-10", "344-2", "466-2", "384-14", "384-6", "472-16", "480-5", "487-6", "480-11", "179-3", "94-4", "179-2", "407-8", "282-2", "19-1", "478-17", "122-13", "494-18", "167-1", "179-4", "342-8", "122-14", "481-6", "168-14", "159-10", "161-4", "177-10", "120-2", "161-2", "172-3", "159-12", "182-4", "180-4", "104-7", "49-17", "489-10", "487-2", "486-11", "486-15", "479-6", "479-2", "476-2", "462-5", "458-9", "438-8", "409-16", "348-25", "203-5", "173-7", "169-5", "105-12", "41-1", "34-5", "1-7", "255-6", "241-1", "206-4", "450-4", "471-2", "191-6", "183-17", "34-15", "469-2", "206-3", "448-4", "451-1", "34-6", "104-10", "35-8", "264-2", "441-1", "472-2", "345-14", "167-3", "406-14", "468-5", "206-2", "491-9", "29-6", "122-18", "47-5", "451-4", "450-1", "471-4", "466-1", "114-4", "429-1", "233-19", "484-21", "35-17", "45-7", "456-2", "424-2", "258-18", "1-1", "483-13", "472-3", "438-7", "441-4", "347-3", "29-2", "169-4", "483-7", "444-2", "454-9", "449-19", "474-15", "489-19", "488-16", "469-4", "480-7", "494-3", "41-15", "439-13", "342-18", "19-5", "267-7", "450-2", "357-13", "255-4", "359-5", "478-14", "191-8", "267-17", "7-7", "438-2", "471-3", "433-4", "24-8", "213-5", "468-12", "466-4", "27-6", "475-1", "361-1", "263-8", "258-20", "213-3", "361-3", "409-15", "113-1", "482-12", "7-2", "183-4", "233-7", "27-5", "27-12", "344-1", "384-10")
# )
```

## Generating Table for Flowering Time
```{r}
# tplant_pop_above_3 <- tplant |> 
#   group_by(Pop) |>
#   slice_sample(n = 10) |>
#   filter(pop_above_3 == T) 

# tplant <- tplant |>
#   mutate(
#     used_for_dormancy = ID %in% dormancy_selected_table_2025_05_14$ID
#   )
# 
# tplant_sampled <- tplant |>
#   filter(pop_above_3 == TRUE) |>
#   group_by(Pop) |>
#   group_modify(~ {
#     dormancy <- filter(.x, used_for_dormancy == TRUE)
#     non_dormancy <- filter(.x, used_for_dormancy == FALSE)
#     needed <- 10 - nrow(dormancy)
#     if (needed > 0) {
#       sampled_non <- slice_sample(non_dormancy, n = needed)
#       bind_rows(dormancy, sampled_non)
#     } else {
#       slice_sample(dormancy, n = 10)
#     }
#   }) |>
#   ungroup()
# 
# n_rows <- 7
# n_cols <- 5
# total_plants <- nrow(tplant_sampled)
# n_trays <- ceiling(total_plants / (n_cols * n_rows))
# pots_per_tray <- n_cols * n_rows
# 
# tplant_F6_random <- tplant_sampled |>
#   select(ID, Pop, Replicate, total_repl_in_pop) |>
#   slice_sample(n = total_plants) |>  # randomization
#   mutate(
#     position = 1:n(),
#     Tray = ceiling(position / pots_per_tray),
#     position_in_tray = (position - 1) %% pots_per_tray,
#     Column = (position_in_tray %% n_cols) + 1,
#     Row = (position_in_tray %/% n_cols) + 1
#   ) |>
#   select(-position, -position_in_tray)  
```


```{r}
# Add the ecosystem type to the tplant df
Phenology <- read_csv(file = "data/Phenology_field_2.csv",
                               col_types = cols(
                                 Site_ID = col_character(),
                                 Type = col_factor(),
                                 First_FT_julianday = col_double()
                               )) |>
  select(Site_ID, Type)

tplant <- tplant |>
  left_join(Phenology, join_by("Pop" == "Site_ID")) |>
  mutate(
    Type = case_when(
      Type == "wall" ~ "Wall",
      Type == "grass" ~ "Grass",
      Type == "pavement" ~ "Pavement",
      TRUE ~ Type
    )
  )
```

## EDA of Leaf Shape and Leaf Edge per Environment
```{r}
Ecotype_colors <- c(
  "wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "grass" = "#6a176e",
  "pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)

# Hist pop per eco type
pop_per_eco <- tplant |>
  distinct(Pop, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  select(Pop, Type) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_pop_per_eco = n())

ggplot(data = pop_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_pop_per_eco, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Number of Populations per Type")
```

```{r}
# Hist replicates per eco type
repl_per_eco <- tplant |>
  select(Replicate, Type) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_repl_per_eco = n())

ggplot(data = repl_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_repl_per_eco, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Total Individuals of the Populations by Type")
```

```{r}
leaf_shape_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_shape) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_shape, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_)

leaf_shape_long <- leaf_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_L, prop_O),
    names_to = "Leaf_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_L" = "#6a176e", "prop_O" = "#f8cd37"),
    labels = c("Elongated", "Oval")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Shape") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_edge) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_)

leaf_edge_long <- leaf_edge_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_G, prop_S),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_edge_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_G" = "#6a176e", "prop_S" = "#f8cd37"),
    labels = c("Not Serrated", "Serrated")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Edge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_shape_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_edge, Leaf_shape) |>
  mutate(Leaf_edge_shape = paste0(Leaf_shape, Leaf_edge)) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge_shape, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_, -prop_Lx)

leaf_edge_shape_long <- leaf_edge_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_LG, prop_LS, prop_OG, prop_OS),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_edge_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_LG" = "#6a176e", "prop_LS" = "#f8cd37",
               "prop_OG" = "#bc3754", "prop_OS" = "#f37819"),
    labels = c("Long Smooth", "Long Serrated", "Oval Smooth", "Oval Serrated")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Edge Shape") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )



```

```{r}
# Hist replicates per eco type
axis_per_eco <- tplant |>
  select(n_shoot_axis, Type) |>
  drop_na(Type, n_shoot_axis) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(mean_axis = round(mean(n_shoot_axis), 1))

ggplot(data = axis_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = mean_axis, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Average Shoot Axis per Type")
```

```{r}
# distribution of sampled pop across ecotpy?
dist_sampled_pop_per_eco <- tplant |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == T,
         sampletype != "Not_sampled") |>
  distinct(Pop, .keep_all = T) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_sampled_pop = n() )

ggplot(data = dist_sampled_pop_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_sampled_pop, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Populations with Leafs Sampled from")
```

## Analysis of Leaf Shape and Leaf Edge of the Transplanted F5 Generation

Since the \~2000 plants are coming from Populations, individuals from the same Population are more similar to each other, creating non-independence. To address this a proportion of the type of leaf edge and shape is calculated per population.

```{r}
# compute a ratio of leaf shape and leaf edge for each population from the individuals in each population
ratio_trait_per_pop <- tplant |>
  drop_na(Leaf_shape, Leaf_edge) |> # remove plants, which could not be categorized
  group_by(Pop, Type) |> # group by Population
  summarise(
    # compute the log ratio of elongated leaf shape over oval leaf shape
    n_L = sum(Leaf_shape == "L"), # sum up the count of elongated leaf per population
    n_O = sum(Leaf_shape == "O"), # sum up the count of oval leaf per population
    # avoid division by 0 by adding 0.01 (laplace smoothing)
    log_ratio_L_O = log((n_L + 0.01) / (n_O + 0.01)),
    # zero means same proportion, positive number means more elongated leaf shape (L), negative oval leaf shape (O)
    
    # compute the log ratio of serrated leaf edge over not serrated leaf edge
    n_S = sum(Leaf_edge == "S"),# sum up the count of serrated leaf per population
    n_G = sum(Leaf_edge == "G"),# sum up the count of not serrated leaf per population
    log_ratio_S_G = log((n_S + 0.01) / (n_G + 0.01)),
    
    # compute the log ratio of plant height
    is_above_18cm = sum(is_above_18cm == T),
    is_below_18cm = sum(is_above_18cm == F),
    log_ratio_plant_heigth = log((is_above_18cm + 0.01) / (is_below_18cm + 0.01))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(Type = as.factor(Type)) 
```


```{r}
ratio_trait_per_pop |>
  select(Type, is_above_18cm, log_ratio_L_O, log_ratio_S_G) |>
  rename(
    Environment = Type,
    Height_log_ratio = is_above_18cm,
    Leaf_Elongation = log_ratio_L_O,
    Leaf_Serration = log_ratio_S_G
  ) |>
  group_by(Environment) |>
  summarise(
    Height_log_ratio = round(mean(Height_log_ratio), 3),
    Leaf_Elongation = round(mean(Leaf_Elongation), 3),
    Leaf_Serration = round(mean(Leaf_Serration), 3)
  ) 
```

(Plants differ in there leaf shape, leaf edge and height phenotypic plasticity across environments. _A. thaliana_ populations from wall environments where taller and showed increased smooth leaf edges when compared to populations growing on pavement. Both showed a similar preference for oval leafs. (Pavement vs Grass) When comparing populations grown on pavement with ones grown on grass, grassy populations tend to be taller, with a stronger tendency to develop oval leafs. There were more populations with serrated leafs on the pavement, also both favor smooth leaf edges overall.)



```{r}
# Are leaf shape and leaf edge correlated and have taller plants elongated leafs?
corr_shape_edge <- glm(log_ratio_L_O ~ log_ratio_S_G + Type + log_ratio_plant_heigth,
                        family = gaussian(),
                        data = ratio_trait_per_pop)
summary(corr_shape_edge)
```
```{r}
plot(corr_shape_edge)
```

The scale-location plot displayed a non-horizontal trend with variance changing systematically across fitted values. Pattern of rising from fitted values of -5 to -2 to then declining observed. homoscedacity violated.
Takle by using robust standard errors:
```{r}
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
robust_results <- coeftest(corr_shape_edge, vcov = vcovHC(corr_shape_edge, type = "HC3"))
robust_results
```

```{r}
summary.lm(corr_shape_edge)
AIC(corr_shape_edge)
```

Confidently observe:
- Leaf shape and edge are correlated (confirmed)
- Taller plants have more elongated leafs
- Env no effect on leaf shape

```{r}
# Leaf shape vs leaf elongation
plot_leaf_shape_vs_elongation <- ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(x = log_ratio_S_G, y = log_ratio_L_O)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, aes(color = Type)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "Population-level Log Serration Ratio", 
       y = "Population-level Log Elongation Ratio") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14))
plot_leaf_shape_vs_elongation
```

```{r}
# Plant height vs leaf elongation
plot_plant_height_vs_elongation <- ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(y = log_ratio_plant_heigth, x = log_ratio_L_O)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, aes(color = Type)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(y = "Population-level Log Height Ratio",
       x = "Population-level Log Elongation Ratio") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14))
plot_plant_height_vs_elongation
```

```{r}
library(patchwork)

# Just combine plots and patchwork handles legend placement beautifully
plot_leaf_shape_vs_elongation + plot_plant_height_vs_elongation +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
```





```{r}
model2 <- step(corr_shape_edge)
summary(model2)
```

Leaf shape and leaf edge are correlated and...

```{r}
ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(x = log_ratio_L_O, y = log_ratio_S_G, color = Type)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~Type) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
cor.test(ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_L_O, ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_plant_heigth)
```
 ...taller plants tend to have more elongated leafs.
 
```{r}
ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(x = log_ratio_L_O, y = log_ratio_plant_heigth)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Type)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Do taller plants have more serrated leafs
serration_model <- glm(log_ratio_S_G ~ log_ratio_plant_heigth + Type,
                        family = gaussian(),
                        data = ratio_trait_per_pop)
summary(serration_model)
summary.lm(serration_model)
```
Indeed, taller plants tend to have more serrated leafs, but it depends on the habitat they grow in. If they grow on Pavement they tend to have more serrated leafs. 

```{r}
plot(serration_model)
```

```{r}
ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(x = log_ratio_S_G, y = log_ratio_plant_heigth)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Type)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(y = "Population-level Log Height Ratio",
       x = "Population-level Log Serration Ratio") +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 18))
```

```{r}
# plot for leaf edge
ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(x = Type, y = log_ratio_S_G, color = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    y = "Population-level Log Serration Ratio",
    x = "Environment Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.position = "none")
```





```{r}
# plot for leaf shape
ggplot(ratio_trait_per_pop, aes(x = Type, y = log_ratio_L_O, color = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    y = "Log Ratio (Elongated/Rounded Leaves)",
    x = "Environment Type"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```


```{r}
# Paiwise Manova Post-Hoc Test with Bonferroni to test if plants with an height above 18 cm, elongated and serrated leafs occur in one of the types of environment more often
Y <- cbind(
  ratio_trait_per_pop$is_above_18cm,
  ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_L_O,
  ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_S_G
)

shapiro_test <- mvnormtest::mshapiro.test(t(Y))
# Mild violation but group size big enough so it's ok

qqnorm(ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_L_O, main = "Q-Q: log_ratio_L_O")
qqline(ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_L_O, col = "red")

qqnorm(ratio_trait_per_pop$is_above_18cm, main = "Q-Q: log_ratio_L_O")
qqline(ratio_trait_per_pop$is_above_18cm, col = "red")

qqnorm(ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_S_G, main = "Q-Q: log_ratio_L_O")
qqline(ratio_trait_per_pop$log_ratio_S_G, col = "red")
# ok'ish qqplots

environments <- unique(ratio_trait_per_pop$Type)
n_env <- length(environments)

pairwise_results <- matrix(NA, n_env, n_env)
rownames(pairwise_results) <- environments
colnames(pairwise_results) <- environments

# Pairwise MANOVA
for (i in 1:(n_env - 1)) {
  for (j in (i + 1):n_env) {
    # Get the two environments to compare
    env1 <- environments[i]
    env2 <- environments[j]
    
    subset_indices <- ratio_trait_per_pop$Type %in% c(env1, env2)
    Y_subset <- Y[subset_indices, ]
    Type_subset <- ratio_trait_per_pop$Type[subset_indices]
    
    pairwise_manova <- manova(Y_subset ~ Type_subset)
    manova_summary <- summary(pairwise_manova)
    p_value <- manova_summary$stats[1, "Pr(>F)"]
    
    pairwise_results[i, j] <- p_value
    pairwise_results[j, i] <- p_value
  }
}

# Apply conservative Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons
n_comparisons <- choose(n_env, 2)  # Number of pairwise comparisons
bonferroni_alpha <- 0.05 / n_comparisons
cat("Bonferroni results of pairwise MANOVA (alpha = ",
    round(bonferroni_alpha, 5),
    "):\n")

for (i in 1:(n_env - 1)) {
  for (j in (i + 1):n_env) {
    env1 <- environments[i]
    env2 <- environments[j]
    p_value <- pairwise_results[i, j]
    
    cat(sprintf("%s vs %s: p = %s", env1, env2, round(p_value, 5)))
    if (p_value < bonferroni_alpha) {
      cat(" *")
    }
    cat("\n")
  }
}
```
```{r}
ratio_trait_per_pop |>
  select(Type, is_above_18cm, log_ratio_L_O, log_ratio_S_G) |>
  rename(
    Environment = Type,
    Height_log_ratio = is_above_18cm,
    Leaf_Elongation = log_ratio_L_O,
    Leaf_Serration = log_ratio_S_G
  ) |>
  group_by(Environment) |>
  summarise(
    Height_log_ratio = round(mean(Height_log_ratio), 3),
    Leaf_Elongation = round(mean(Leaf_Elongation), 3),
    Leaf_Serration = round(mean(Leaf_Serration), 3)
  ) 
```


# F6 Experiment
Load in the table containing the random subsample of the urban cologne F5 line, which will be used in the following experiment. The table contains plants used for a new F6 generation. New ID's where given to the plants.

```{r}
# Table for the plants that are sown to produce an F6 generation.
# Plants were chosen from populations with more than 3 individuals and reduced to not carry more than 10 individuals, based on the information at hand at that time. If an individual was not at hand it was replaced by an individual at hand at random, producing the table.
tplant_F6_random <- read_csv(file = "data/tplant_F6_random_2025_06_03.csv",
                             na = "na",
                             col_types = cols(
                               ID = col_character(),
                               ID_F5_parent = col_character(),
                               Pop = col_character(),
                               Individuum = col_character(),
                               Tray = col_character(),
                               Column = col_character(),
                               Row = col_character(),
                               not_germinated_til = col_date()
                             )) 
```

```{r}
# not_germinated <- read_csv(file = "data/not_germinated_2025_06_02.csv")
# tplant_F6_random2 <- tplant_F6_random |>
#   mutate(germination_date = case_when(
#     ID %in% not_germinated$not_germinated ~ not_germinated$date,
#     TRUE ~ "not_germinated"
#   ))
```
```{r}
tplant_F6_random |>
  drop_na(not_germinated_til)
```


## Load Dormancy Count Table
```{r}
# Count data of germination rate of seeds from the F5 generation
dormancy_count <- read_csv(file = "data/dormancy count_2025_06_11.csv",
                           na = "na",
                           col_types = cols(
                             ID_F5_parent = col_character(),
                             seed_count_germinated = col_integer(),
                             seed_count_total = col_integer(),
                             has_fungi = col_logical(),
                             has_strong_fungi = col_logical(),
                             dormtest_start = col_date(),
                             dormtest_end = col_date(),
                             group = col_integer(),
                             run = col_integer(),
                             Notes = col_character()
                           )) |>
  mutate(ID_F5_parent = case_when(ID_F5_parent == "165-4" ~ "156-4",
                                  ID_F5_parent == "165-8" ~ "156-8",
                                  ID_F5_parent == "165-17" ~ "156-17",
                                  TRUE ~ ID_F5_parent))

# find duplicates
# dormancy_count$ID_F5_parent[duplicated(dormancy_count$ID_F5_parent)]

# add new F6 ID to the table
dormancy_count <- dormancy_count |>
  left_join(tplant_F6_random |> dplyr::select(ID, ID_F5_parent), join_by("ID_F5_parent"))
```

```{r}
# get germination ratio
dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi <- dormancy_count |>
  filter(has_strong_fungi == FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    germ_ratio = case_when(
      seed_count_germinated == 0 ~ 0,
      seed_count_germinated != 0 ~ seed_count_germinated / (seed_count_total - seed_count_germinated)
    )
  )
```


```{r}
hist(dormancy_count$seed_count_germinated)
```

```{r}
hist(dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi$germ_ratio)
```
